name: Lowrance HDS LIVE
searchTerms: lowrance hds live memory card size
lowrance hds live sd card not reading
whySpecs: The HDS LIVE only supports FAT32-formatted SDHC cards, which top out at 32GB by spec. Cards larger than that ship as exFAT/SDXC and the unit won't read them — this is a hardware/firmware limitation of the SD controller, not a defect.

faq.q: What happens if I use a card bigger than 32GB in a Lowrance HDS LIVE?
faq.a: It won't be recognized, or a software update may fail to read it. This isn't damage to the unit or the card — swap in a 32GB or smaller card formatted as FAT32 and it will work normally.

notes: Original brief claimed oversized cards cause 'fatal update loops' — no credible source supports that. Verified failure mode is simple non-recognition/failed reads, fully recoverable by using a compliant card. Toned down accordingly.

name: Garmin ECHOMAP UHD
searchTerms: garmin echomap uhd memory card size
garmin echomap uhd max sd card
whySpecs: Garmin's owner's manual caps supported cards at 32GB, formatted FAT32. The card stores map data plus sonar logs, waypoints, routes, and screenshots — not maps exclusively.

faq.q: Is the SD card in the ECHOMAP UHD only used for maps?
faq.a: No — the card also stores sonar logs, waypoints, routes, and screenshots, not just map data. Keep that in mind when deciding how much free space to leave available beyond whatever charts you load.

name: Humminbird Helix 7
whySpecs: The Helix 7 has a single microSD slot capped at 32GB for chart and sonar-log storage.

faq.q: Does the Helix 7 have two SD card slots?
faq.a: No — the Helix 7 has a single microSD slot, unlike some other Helix models that do have two. Don't assume dual-slot behavior (like simultaneous chart + backup cards) on this model.

notes: Original brief claimed dual card slots on the Helix 7 — corrected to single slot per Humminbird documentation. Some other Helix models do have two slots; the 7 does not.
